mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid spike in the unemployment rate triggers risk-off positioning as investors price in imminent recessionary earnings cuts, depressing S&P 500 prices within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.25 percentage points in 30 days
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in consumer credit and housing starts signals a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue growth expectations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive when both credit and housing metrics decline concurrently for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing labor cost pressures boosts corporate profit margins, driving equity outperformance over a one-month horizon when capacity utilization remains elevated.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 during periods where industrial output rises while employment growth stagnates.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate signals robust aggregate demand, providing a positive earnings momentum signal that lifts equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant difference between high-GDP-growth and low-GDP-growth regimes over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates persistently above 4.5% sustain high borrowing costs that suppress equity multiples and delay capital expenditure cycles, weighing on quarterly market performance.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand by more than 5% over 60 days while the policy rate remains above the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable unemployment rate indicate a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 declines for more than 55% of months where payroll growth exceeds 150,000 and unemployment remains below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts during a peak economic cycle precede a sharp reversal in consumer confidence and discretionary spending, triggering a short-term equity drawdown within five trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts surge while S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 70% of observed instances.
